edges.description: Disruption of Commander assembly impairs the SNX17-dependent retrieval and recycling of integral membrane cargo proteins from endosomes to the cell surface.
Impaired recycling reduces tissue-specific presentation of cell-surface integral membrane proteins essential for kidney, bone, and brain development, and reduces surface lipoprotein receptors.
Tissue-specific loss of surface cargo during organogenesis produces cerebellar, cardiac, craniofacial, renal, and skeletal malformations.

nodes.description: Atrial septal defect is among the recurrent congenital heart defects in RSS.

evidence.reference_title: Ritscher-Schinzel Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Standard treatment for obesity, obstructive sleep apnea, cleft palate, congenital heart defects, hypercholesterolemia, renal anomalies, immunodeficiency, and developmental delay / intellectual disability.

nodes.description: Hypoplasia of the cerebellar vermis is part of the posterior fossa anomaly spectrum in RSS.

evidence.reference_title: Missense variant in CCDC22 causes X-linked recessive intellectual disability with features of Ritscher-Schinzel/3C syndrome.
evidence.snippet: characterized by intellectual disability, cerebellar brain malformations, congenital heart defects, and craniofacial abnormalities

nodes.description: High-arched or cleft palate is a recurrent craniofacial feature in RSS.

evidence.reference_title: Ritscher-Schinzel Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: smooth or short philtrum, and high or cleft palate

nodes.description: Pathogenic variants in Commander and WASH complex genes (WASHC5, CCDC22, VPS35L, COMMD4, COMMD9, CCDC93) destabilize or disrupt assembly of the 16-subunit Commander assembly (Retriever: VPS35L/VPS26C/VPS29 plus the CCC complex: COMMD1-10 with CCDC22 and CCDC93) and the functionally coupled WASH complex. Most variants are loss-of-function or complex-destabilizing, reducing endosomal recycling capacity.

evidence.reference_title: Structure of the endosomal Commander complex linked to Ritscher-Schinzel syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The Commander complex is required for endosomal recycling of diverse transmembrane cargos and is mutated in Ritscher-Schinzel syndrome. It comprises two sub-assemblies: Retriever composed of VPS35L, VPS26C, and VPS29; and the CCC complex which contains twelve subunits: COMMD1-COMMD10 and the coiled-coil domain-containing (CCDC) proteins CCDC22 and CCDC93.

evidence.reference_title: CCDC22 mutations that impair COMMD binding cause attenuated 3C/Ritscher-Schinzel syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in genes encoding subunits of these three complexes, CCDC22, VPS35L, and WASHC5, have been linked with a developmental syndrome known as 3 C (cranio-cerebello-cardiac) or Ritscher-Schinzel syndrome.

nodes.description: Congenital cardiac malformations, including atrial and ventricular septal defects, atrioventricular canal defects, and tetralogy of Fallot, are the cardiac component of the 3C triad and are reported in the majority of cases.

evidence.reference_title: Ritscher-Schinzel Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Ritscher-Schinzel syndrome (RSS) is a clinically recognizable condition that includes the cardinal findings of craniofacial features, cerebellar defects, and cardiovascular malformations resulting in the alternate diagnostic name of 3C syndrome.

nodes.description: Characteristic craniofacial features include brachycephaly, hypotonic face with protruding tongue, short midface, widely spaced eyes, downslanted palpebral fissures, low-set ears, smooth or short philtrum, and high or cleft palate.

evidence.reference_title: Ritscher-Schinzel Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Dysmorphic facial features may include brachycephaly, hypotonic face with protruding tongue, flat appearance of the face on profile view, short midface, widely spaced eyes, downslanted palpebral fissures, low-set ears with overfolding of the upper helix, smooth or short philtrum, and high or cleft palate.

nodes.description: Cerebellar malformation characteristic of the RSS triad, part of the spectrum of cerebellar/posterior fossa anomalies.

evidence.reference_title: Missense variant in CCDC22 causes X-linked recessive intellectual disability with features of Ritscher-Schinzel/3C syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Ritscher-Schinzel syndrome (RSS)/3C (cranio-cerebro-cardiac) syndrome (OMIM#220210) is a rare and clinically heterogeneous developmental disorder characterized by intellectual disability, cerebellar brain malformations, congenital heart defects, and craniofacial abnormalities.

nodes.description: Downslanted palpebral fissures are part of the characteristic facial gestalt of RSS.

evidence.reference_title: Ritscher-Schinzel Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: widely spaced eyes, downslanted palpebral fissures, low-set ears with overfolding of the upper helix

nodes.description: Ocular anomalies (including coloboma per Orphanet) may be variably present in RSS.

evidence.reference_title: Ritscher-Schinzel Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Eye anomalies and hypercholesterolemia may be variably present.

nodes.description: Developmental delay is a near-universal feature; in a WASHC5-associated cohort it was reported in all assessed cases.

evidence.reference_title: Ritscher-Schinzel Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: RSS is associated with variable degrees of developmental delay and intellectual disability.

nodes.description: Hypercholesterolemia may be variably present and is a confirmed complication of VPS35L-associated RSS, mechanistically linked to reduced surface lipoprotein receptors.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical diversity and molecular mechanism of VPS35L-associated Ritscher-Schinzel syndrome.
evidence.snippet: In addition to typical features of RSS, we confirmed hypercholesterolaemia, hypogammaglobulinaemia and intestinal lymphangiectasia as novel complications of VPS35L-associated RSS.

nodes.description: Widely spaced eyes (hypertelorism) is a recurrent craniofacial feature of RSS.

evidence.reference_title: Ritscher-Schinzel Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: short midface, widely spaced eyes, downslanted palpebral fissures, low-set ears with overfolding of the upper helix

nodes.description: Decreased circulating immunoglobulin (hypogammaglobulinemia) is a novel complication confirmed in VPS35L-associated RSS.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical diversity and molecular mechanism of VPS35L-associated Ritscher-Schinzel syndrome.
evidence.snippet: In addition to typical features of RSS, we confirmed hypercholesterolaemia, hypogammaglobulinaemia and intestinal lymphangiectasia as novel complications of VPS35L-associated RSS.

nodes.description: Commander organizes the sorting nexin-17 (SNX17)-dependent recycling of hundreds of integral membrane proteins through the endosomal network. Commander dysfunction reduces cell-surface presentation of cargos bearing SNX17-recognized ΦxNPxY/F or ΦxNxxY/F sorting motifs, including integrins and lipoprotein receptors, in a tissue-specific manner.

biological_processes: endosome to plasma membrane protein transport

evidence.reference_title: Ritscher-Schinzel syndrome can be characterized as an endosomal recyclinopathy.
evidence.snippet: Commander organizes the sorting nexin-17 (SNX17)-dependent recycling of hundreds of integral membrane proteins through the endosomal network.

evidence.reference_title: Ritscher-Schinzel syndrome can be characterized as an endosomal recyclinopathy.
evidence.snippet: these integral proteins contained ΦxNPxY/F or ΦxNxxY/F sorting motifs in their cytoplasmic-facing domains (where Φ is a hydrophobic residue and x is any residue) that are recognized by SNX17 to drive their Commander-dependent endosomal recycling

nodes.description: RSS is associated with variable degrees of developmental delay and intellectual disability.

evidence.reference_title: Ritscher-Schinzel Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: RSS is associated with variable degrees of developmental delay and intellectual disability.

nodes.description: Intestinal lymphangiectasia is a novel complication confirmed in VPS35L-associated RSS.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical diversity and molecular mechanism of VPS35L-associated Ritscher-Schinzel syndrome.
evidence.snippet: In addition to typical features of RSS, we confirmed hypercholesterolaemia, hypogammaglobulinaemia and intestinal lymphangiectasia as novel complications of VPS35L-associated RSS.

nodes.description: The convergent consequence of impaired Commander-dependent recycling is a congenital multiple-organ malformation syndrome affecting the cerebellum (Dandy-Walker malformation, vermis hypoplasia), heart (septal and atrioventricular canal defects, tetralogy of Fallot), craniofacial structures, kidney (proteinuria), liver, and skeleton, together with neurodevelopmental impairment.

evidence.reference_title: Ritscher-Schinzel syndrome can be characterized as an endosomal recyclinopathy.
evidence.snippet: Ritscher-Schinzel syndrome (RSS) is a congenital malformation syndrome characterized by cerebellar, cardiac, and craniofacial malformations and phenotypes associated with liver, skeletal, and kidney dysfunction.

nodes.description: Obesity is a recognized manifestation of RSS addressed in clinical management per GeneReviews.

evidence.reference_title: Ritscher-Schinzel Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Standard treatment for obesity, obstructive sleep apnea, cleft palate, congenital heart defects, hypercholesterolemia, renal anomalies, immunodeficiency, and developmental delay / intellectual disability.

nodes.description: Obstructive sleep apnea is a recognized manifestation of RSS addressed in clinical management per GeneReviews.

evidence.reference_title: Ritscher-Schinzel Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Standard treatment for obesity, obstructive sleep apnea, cleft palate, congenital heart defects, hypercholesterolemia, renal anomalies, immunodeficiency, and developmental delay / intellectual disability.

nodes.description: Proteinuria reflects renal involvement in RSS and is recapitulated in mouse models of the disease.

evidence.reference_title: Ritscher-Schinzel syndrome can be characterized as an endosomal recyclinopathy.
evidence.snippet: through generation of mouse models of RSS, we show replication of RSS-associated clinical phenotypes including proteinuria, skeletal malformation, and neurological impairment

nodes.description: Defective recycling lowers steady-state cell-surface levels of receptors and adhesion molecules. In VPS35L-associated RSS, ablation decreases surface LRP1 and LDLR, reducing LDL uptake and providing a mechanism for hypercholesterolemia. Reduced tissue-specific surface cargo presentation drives the multisystem (cerebellar, cardiac, craniofacial, renal, skeletal) developmental defects.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical diversity and molecular mechanism of VPS35L-associated Ritscher-Schinzel syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Cellular analysis found VPS35L ablation decreased the cell surface level of lipoprotein receptor-related protein 1 and low-density lipoprotein receptor, resulting in reduced low-density lipoprotein cellular uptake.

evidence.reference_title: Ritscher-Schinzel syndrome can be characterized as an endosomal recyclinopathy.
evidence.snippet: through cell surface proteomics, that this reduces tissue-specific presentation of cell surface integral membrane proteins essential for kidney, bone, and brain development
